---
title: ""
---

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="68%"}

## Robert Annabel

**Lecturer**, School of Economics, University of Sydney

[r.w.b.annabel@gmail.com](mailto:r.w.b.annabel@gmail.com) &nbsp;·&nbsp; [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/robert-annabel/)

---

I am a Lecturer in the School of Economics at the University of Sydney. I hold a PhD in Economics from UNSW Business School (2025), supervised by Professor Arghya Ghosh and Associate Professor Anthony Asher.

My research sits at the intersection of financial economics, industrial organisation theory, and econometric methods. I study how institutional investors — superannuation funds, index funds, and large asset managers — shape the behaviour of the firms they own, with implications for corporate governance, market competition, and sustainability outcomes.

I currently teach Financial Economics (ECON6021) and coordinate the Industrial Organisation stream of the Economics Research Project (ECON7030) at the postgraduate level.

:::

::: {.column width="4%"}
:::

::: {.column width="28%"}

![](headshot.JPG){.headshot fig-alt="Robert Annabel"}

:::

::::
